subtitle: When the Wooden Capital Faced the Western World

panels.shot: epic low-angle wide of city and river
panels.action: Megasthenes stands on a timber rampart above a broad river confluence, cloaked and watchful, as the vast wooden palisade and towers of Pataliputra stretch away into the smoky, dawn-lit distance.

dialogue.text: In the age of Chandragupta, when the Ganges met the Son, a Greek named Megasthenes walked the wooden capital and carried India’s image to a distant world.

i18n.hi: चंद्रगुप्त के युग में, जहाँ गंगा सोन् से मिली, वहीं यूनानी मेगास्थनीज़ लकड़ी की राजधानी में चला और भारत की छवि दूर जगत तक ले गया।

panels.shot: bird’s-eye wide over caravan and river plain
panels.action: A small Greek-led caravan winds along a dusty track beside a shining river, with Megasthenes riding under a parasol, pointing ahead as haze reveals faint lines of walls on the far horizon.

dialogue.text: From the lands once ruled by Alexander, Megasthenes rode east through heat and dust, following rivers and roads into the heart of Chandragupta’s empire.

i18n.hi: अलेक्ज़ैंडर की पुरानी रियासतों से निकलकर मेगास्थनीज़ धूप और धूल चीरता पूर्व की ओर बढ़ा, नदियों और सड़कों के सहारे चंद्रगुप्त के साम्राज्य के दिल तक।

panels.shot: medium profile shot beside Indian guide on the road
panels.action: Megasthenes rides alongside a Mauryan guide in a simple cotton wrap, both silhouetted against the flat river plain as distant forests fringe the horizon; Megasthenes holds a wax tablet and stylus in his hand.

dialogue.speaker: megasthenes
dialogue.text: How far yet to Palibothra, this wooden capital of yours?

i18n.hi: तुम्हारी लकड़ी की राजधानी पालीबोथ्रा अभी कितनी दूर है?

panels.shot: wide establishing shot of confluence and city outline
panels.action: At sunset, Megasthenes and his escort crest a low rise to see the broad Ganges and the shifting channels of the Son below, and beyond them, the long dark line of Pataliputra’s timber walls hugging the riverbank.

dialogue.text: Where the Son once joined the Ganges, earth and water embraced a city. There, Pataliputra stretched like a long parallelogram along the river’s curve.

i18n.hi: जहाँ सोन् कभी गंगा से मिला, वहीं मिट्टी और पानी ने एक नगर को थाम लिया। वहीं पटलीपुत्र नदी की वक्र रेखा पर लंबे चतुर्भुज‑सा फैल गया।

panels.shot: low-angle close-wide from moat up to wall and towers
panels.action: Seen from the brink of a broad ditch, the massive wooden palisade of Pataliputra rises, thick beams dark with age, pierced by arrow slits, its line broken by watchtowers under the smoky evening sky.

dialogue.text: Megasthenes counted beams and bastions. A deep ditch, a timber wall with slits for archers, towers marching in line, and gates at measured intervals guarded the king’s capital.

i18n.hi: मेगास्थनीज़ ने शहतीरों और बुर्जों की गिनती की। गहरी खाई, तीरंदाज़ी के छेदों वाली लकड़ी की दीवार, कतार में बढ़ते मीनार, और तय फ़ासलों पर फाटकों की रखवाली में लगा राजधर्म।

panels.shot: architectural overhead diagram-like wide of wall section
panels.action: From above, a stretch of palisade is seen: massive squared wooden posts set close together, braced by inner beams, a walkway atop with sentries, the ditch outside gleaming with still water and reeds.

dialogue.text: He would later tell the Greeks of a city girded with a palisade of timber, of hundreds of towers and dozens of gates, each placed with soldier’s care along the long walls.

i18n.hi: बाद में उसने यूनानियों को उस नगरी का वर्णन किया, जो लकड़ी की बेरी में घिरी थी—सैकड़ों मीनार, दर्जनों फाटक, हर एक सैनिकों की सूझ‑बूझ से लंबी दीवारों पर जड़ा हुआ।

panels.shot: medium at city gate from inside the ditch
panels.action: At a wooden gatehouse, Mauryan soldiers in simple tunics and turbans stand with spears and bows, as Megasthenes, in Greek cloak and sandals, dismounts and offers a sealed letter to an officer.

dialogue.text: By treaty and diplomacy he came, bearing Seleukos’ words to Chandragupta. Even so, the gate captain read, weighed, and measured before he opened the way.

i18n.hi: वह संधि और कूटनीति के सहारे आया, सेल्युकस का संदेश लेकर चंद्रगुप्त के पास। फिर भी, द्वारपाल ने पढ़ा, परखा, तौला, तब कहीं राह खोली।

panels.shot: tracking wide shot as he enters through the gate
panels.action: Megasthenes walks under the shadowed timber gate, glancing up at the heavy beams above, while carts creak past and a line of elephants waits within, their silhouettes filling the crowded passage.

dialogue.speaker: megasthenes
dialogue.text: A city of wood, yet stronger than many of stone.

i18n.hi: लकड़ी का नगर, पर कई पत्थर के नगरों से ज़्यादा सख्त।

panels.shot: street-level wide shot of interior city avenue
panels.action: Inside the walls, Megasthenes walks along a broad street flanked by wooden houses and courtyards, with traders, scribes, ox-carts, and children moving amid trees and smoke rising from clay hearths.

dialogue.text: Within the palisade lay a living maze of streets and courtyards, storehouses and gardens, where clerks counted grain and soldiers drilled, and the pulse of an empire beat in dust and voices.

i18n.hi: उस बेरी के भीतर गलियों और आँगनों, गोदामों और बागों की धड़कती भूलभुलैया थी, जहाँ मुनीम अनाज गिनते, सैनिक कसरत करते, और साम्राज्य की धड़कन धूल और आवाज़ों में चलती।

panels.shot: medium shot of Megasthenes sketching on tablet in a courtyard
panels.action: Seated in a shaded courtyard near a lotus pond, Megasthenes rests his tablet on his knee, carefully scratching lines that outline the city’s long shape and walls, while a Mauryan official gestures to a map on the ground.

dialogue.text: He drew Pataliputra in careful strokes. A city long, not round; flanked by rivers, ringed by defenses, ordered by royal will and the needs of war and grain.

i18n.hi: उसने पटलीपुत्र को बारीक रेखाओं में उकेरा—लंबा, गोल नहीं; नदियों से सटा, क़िलेबंद घेरा लिए, राजइच्छा और युद्ध‑अनाज की ज़रूरतों से सधा।

panels.shot: majestic low-angle wide of palace exterior across water
panels.action: Across a reflecting pool, the royal complex rises, its elevated wooden halls and balconies supported by rows of columns, with carved railings and gardens, as Megasthenes approaches with a small escort.

dialogue.text: Beyond the common streets stood the king’s palace, its timber raised upon pillars, its halls set above pools and gardens. To Megasthenes, it rivalled the courts of Susa and Ecbatana.

i18n.hi: साधारण गलियों से परे राजा का महल था—स्तंभों पर उठी लकड़ी, तालाबों और बागों के ऊपर टिके दीवानख़ाने। मेगास्थनीज़ को यह सूसा और एकबातना के दरबारों की टक्कर का लगा।

panels.shot: interior wide of pillared hall from low angle
panels.action: Inside a vast pillared hall, lines of polished wooden columns rise in regular rows, their capitals simply carved, as courtiers stand aside and Megasthenes is led beneath the high ceiling toward a distant throne.

dialogue.text: He walked through a forest of pillars. Later ages would dig the earth here and find stone bases of such columns, silent witnesses to the halls he praised.

i18n.hi: वह स्तंभों के जंगल से गुज़रा। बाद की सदियाँ यहीं मिट्टी खोदेंगी और इन्हीं खंभों के पत्थर के आधार ढूँढेंगी—उन दीवारों के गूँगे गवाह, जिनकी उसने प्रशंसा की थी।

panels.shot: medium close-up on Megasthenes’ face as he looks up
panels.action: Megasthenes stands still in the pillared hall, eyes lifted in quiet awe, light filtering down onto his face as attendants in fine cotton and jewelry wait in ordered rows behind him.

dialogue.speaker: megasthenes
dialogue.text: This court is no barbarian tent, but a kingdom of law.

i18n.hi: यह दरबार किसी जंगली का तंबू नहीं, बल्कि क़ानून से चलने वाला राज।

panels.shot: medium two-shot of Megasthenes before Mauryan official
panels.action: In a side chamber lined with reed documents and wooden boards, Megasthenes speaks calmly to a dignified Mauryan minister, his hands open as he explains his king’s message, while scribes record their words.

dialogue.text: Between Seleukos and Chandragupta lay war once, then agreement. Now their men spoke of elephants and borders, of marriage ties and tribute, in the cool shadow of the timber palace.

i18n.hi: सेल्युकस और चंद्रगुप्त के बीच कभी युद्ध था, फिर समझौता। अब उनके लोग इसी लकड़ी के महल की ठंडी छाया में हाथियों, सरहदों, शादियों और नज़राने की बातें करते थे।

panels.shot: nighttime wide shot of ramparts overlooking river
panels.action: At night, Megasthenes stands again upon the rampart, watching torches move along the wall and small boats glide on the dark river, his cloak drawn close against the cool breeze.

dialogue.text: He measured not only walls and halls, but vigilance. Watchfires burned along the ramparts; patrols paced; beyond, the river carried whispers of distant uplands and seas.

i18n.hi: उसने सिर्फ दीवारें और दीवान नहीं, चौकसी भी नापी। प्राचीरों पर मशालें जलतीं, गश्ती दल चक्कर लगाते; और पार बहती नदी दूर के पहाड़ों और समुद्रों की फुसफुसाहट ढोती।

panels.shot: tight close-up on Megasthenes’ hand writing on a scroll
panels.action: By a small oil lamp, Megasthenes’ fingers guide a stylus over a fresh scroll, lines of Greek letters forming as he pauses between phrases, wax tablets and rough sketches spread beside him.

dialogue.text: In that wooden capital he began the work that would outlive him, shaping his Indica, trying to catch in words a land strange to his readers and larger than his sight.

i18n.hi: उसी लकड़ी की राजधानी में उसने अपने ‘इंडिका’ की नींव रखी—एक ऐसी भूमि को शब्दों में पकड़ने की कोशिश में, जो उसके पाठकों के लिए अनजानी और उसकी नज़र से भी बड़ी थी।

panels.shot: montage-style wide showing imagined future Greek readers around a scroll
panels.action: Centuries later in a dim Greek or Roman study, scholars bend over a worn scroll, one reading aloud as others listen, fragments of Megasthenes’ words visible before them, shelves of other texts rising behind.

dialogue.text: His book would be copied, shortened, quarrelled over. Some called him careless; yet others, and our own age, sift his lines like dust to find grains of truth about Mauryan India.

i18n.hi: उसकी किताब नकल हुई, घटाई गई, और विवादों में घिरी। कुछ ने उसे ढीला कहा; पर औरों ने, और हमारी सदी ने, उसकी पंक्तियों को धूल‑सा छानकर मौर्यकालीन भारत की सच्चाई के कण चुने।

panels.shot: archaeological bird’s-eye of excavation at Bulandibagh and Kumrahar
panels.action: Modern archaeologists stand in a waterlogged trench, examining dark ancient wooden beams in the soil at Bulandibagh, while nearby at Kumrahar, exposed stone pillar bases form a solemn grid in the sunlit earth.

dialogue.text: When diggers at Bulandibagh unearthed buried palisade beams, when Kumrahar’s pillared hall rose again in plan, their spades brushed against the lines Megasthenes once wrote of towers, ditches and royal halls.

i18n.hi: जब बुलंदीबाग के खुदाइयों ने दबी हुई लकड़ी की बेरी के शहतीर निकाले, जब कुम्हरार का स्तंभ‑हॉल नक्शों में फिर खड़ा हुआ, तब उनकी कुदालें उन ही मीनारों, खाइयों और राजमहलों की लकीरों से टकराईं, जिनका ज़िक्र मेगास्थनीज़ ने किया था।

panels.shot: final wide shot of Megasthenes silhouetted on the wall at dawn
panels.action: At pale dawn, Megasthenes stands alone atop the wooden wall, the vast plain and braided rivers stretching away, as birds rise from the moat and the city slowly wakes behind him.

dialogue.text: So the Greek envoy on the wooden wall became a bridge of memory. His gaze, flawed yet priceless, lets us see Pataliputra’s strength, and warns us to weigh every stranger’s story with care.

i18n.hi: यूँ लकड़ी की दीवार पर खड़ा वह यूनानी दूत यादों का पुल बन गया। उसकी नज़र, ख़ामीदार पर अनमोल, हमें पटलीपुत्र की ताक़त दिखाती है—और हर अजनबी की कहानी को तौलकर मानने की सीख देती है।

subtitle_i18n.en: When the Wooden Capital Faced the Western World
subtitle_i18n.hi: जब लकड़ी की राजधानी ने पश्चिम से टक्कर ली
